text: Daniel, my NUTR 301 lecture runs long Thursday. Can you take the first 15 minutes of the lab so students can start the diet recall exercise?
Happy to. I'll have them pull up the 24-hour recall worksheet and pair off before you get there.
Thanks. Tell them to use the food models on the back shelf for portion estimation. Last cohort guessed everything by eye and the data was a mess.
Good call. I'll set the models out before they arrive. See you at the break.
Heads up, I moved the community nutrition guest lecture to week 9. The clinic dietitian I lined up had a scheduling conflict.
Noted. I'll update the syllabus on Classroom so the students see the new date.
For the NUTR 411 exam, are we keeping the case study on medical nutrition therapy for Type 2 diabetes? I want to write the rubric around it.
Yes, keep it. The diabetes case mapped to the learning objectives better than the renal one. Send me the rubric draft and I'll add the grading weights.
Will do tonight. I'd weight the nutrition diagnosis section heaviest, that's where they lose the most points.
Agreed. They rush the PES statement every year. Let's make it 30 percent.
Reminder for all faculty: spring textbook orders are due to the bookstore by Friday. NUTR 301 and 411 lists have not changed from last term.
Mine are in. Same edition for both courses, no swaps.
Adjunct contracts for spring went out this morning. Please review the section times and let me know by next week if anything conflicts.
Mine looks right. I have NUTR 301 Tuesday and Thursday mornings, which keeps my clinical days at Ashfield clear in the afternoon.
Same here, the evening 411 section works. Thanks for keeping the adjunct slots consistent this year.
IRB approved the culturally adapted diabetes intervention study. We can start participant recruitment in January.
Excellent. I'll set up the screening tracker. How many participants are we targeting for the first cohort?
Forty for the pilot, recruited through the community clinics. I'll share the inclusion criteria doc this afternoon.
Forty is realistic for a pilot. I'll get the consent forms into Box once you confirm the criteria.
I need a TA for the NUTR 301 Thursday lab section this spring. Two sections of 24 each, mostly the diet analysis software.
I have a strong second-year grad student who knows the software well. I'll send you her info and you can interview her this week.
That would help a lot. Thursday afternoons work for me between clinic and pickup. Send it over.
Curriculum committee is reviewing the NUTR 411 reading list. I want to add two recent papers on community dietary interventions, can I bring them Friday?
Yes, bring them. The current list leans heavy on clinical and light on community, so that fills a gap.
Good. I'll pull the citations and a one paragraph rationale for each so the committee can vote without a long discussion.
Perfect. If you send them by Wednesday I'll add them to the agenda packet.
Grades are submitted for both 301 sections. The diet analysis project scores were the strongest I have seen, the new rubric helped.
Mine too. Spelling out the nutrition diagnosis criteria up front made a real difference in their write-ups.
Let's keep the rubric for fall. I'll add a short worked case so they see what a complete PES statement looks like.
Spring faculty meeting is moved to the 18th at 3pm in the conference room. Agenda will go out Monday.
I can make 3pm. I have clinic until 1 that day so the timing works.
Same, I'll be there. Can we add the spring textbook reconciliation to the agenda?
